disclaimer: This tool is for clinical education and decision support only. It is not a diagnostic device and must not be used for autonomous patient care. All recommendations require independent clinical verification by a qualified clinician.

ranked_mechanisms.label: True acid GERD with competent peristalsis

insufficient_inputs: Esophageal biopsies (eosinophil count) not documented despite dysphagia or PPI nonresponse. EoE cannot be excluded — obtain >=6 biopsies from proximal + distal esophagus per ACG/AGREE.

first_line: PPI once daily 30–60 min before meals x 8 weeks (escalate to BID if partial response)
Confirm PPI adherence and timing before declaring failure
escalation: Add H2RA as needed when breakthrough symptoms occur
Add alginate after meals and at bedtime for postprandial acid pocket
On-PPI pH-impedance if refractory to confirm whether ongoing symptoms are acid, non-acid, or functional

procedural.label: Nissen (complete 360°) fundoplication
procedural.indication: Objective GERD with intact peristalsis; failed or intolerant of medical therapy

procedural.label: Toupet (partial posterior 270°) fundoplication
procedural.indication: Alternative wrap with lower postoperative dysphagia risk; acceptable in intact peristalsis

procedural.label: Transoral incisionless fundoplication (TIF)
procedural.indication: Hiatal hernia <= 2 cm AND LA grade <= B

procedural.label: Concomitant hiatal hernia repair + TIF (c-TIF)
procedural.indication: Hiatal hernia > 2 cm and < 4 cm AND LA grade < C

lifestyle: No oral intake for 3 hours before lying supine
Avoid individual trigger foods (e.g. fatty/spicy foods, caffeine, alcohol, chocolate, mint)
